floor_history.headline: recon: sibling-proven one-probe close -- saTan2InfoInit_80044F80's clean two-param form compiles to the byte-identical shape (only the addiu immediate differs, 0x4D vs 0x27); rewrite func_80044F30 as

frontier.hypothesis: The clean two-param rewrite `void func_80044F30(s32 a0, s32 a1) { func_80044E74(a0 + 0x27, a1); }` scores sandbox 0 (proven by the byte-identical sibling saTan2InfoInit_80044F80 whose source is exactly this shape)
frontier.mechanism: calls.c expand_call: second arg already lives in $a1 so no caller-side insn is emitted; jal delay-slot fill of the addiu by reorg.c fill_simple_delay_slots is identical to the sibling's
frontier.next_probe: apply the rewrite + update include/m2c_context.h:601 to (s32, s32); sandbox func_80044F30 expecting 0, then full build to confirm no other TU regresses on the prototype change

frontier.hypothesis: Updating m2c_context.h:601 is byte-neutral for every other TU (the header is context-only; the sole cross-TU callers in text1a_c2.c already assume two params)
frontier.mechanism: prototype changes alter caller codegen only where arg counts/types change materially; text1a_c2.c call sites already pass two s32 args, so their RTL is unchanged
frontier.next_probe: grep for any other #include consumer declaring or calling func_80044F30, then verify-oracle after the edit (driver-side); expected no-op outside text1a_c.c

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Function-pointer cast cheat: definition declares (s32 a0) with ((void (*)(s32))func_80044E74)(a0+0x27), but callers (text1a_c2.c:14,77,79) declare and call it as func_80044F30(s32, s32) — two explicit args. The cast exists solely to compensate for the missing second parameter declaration, relying on MIPS $a1 register passthrough. Sibling saTan2InfoInit_80044F80 proves the clean form works: void func_80044F30(s32 a0, s32 a1) { func_80044E74(a0+0x27, a1); } produces identical target asm without any cast. Worker must rewrite with two-param signature, drop the cast, and update m2c_context.h:601 accordingly.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
